topics.summary: With 17 days until the June 3 local elections, polls show DPK leading in Seoul (Jeong Won-o 46% vs. PPP's Oh Se-hun 38%) but margins narrowing sharply in Busan and Daegu to within the margin of error. PPP party approval has risen four consecutive weeks to 23%, while DPK holds 45%.

evidence_headlines.title: 6·3 지선 격전지 여야 격차 줄어…서울·부산·대구 등 판세 주목

topics.summary: On May 15, the special counsel demanded a 7-year-6-month sentence for former first lady Kim Keon-hee on charges of receiving luxury gifts in exchange for public appointment favors; the court set a sentencing date of June 26, just one week before the local elections. Kim apologized in her final statement but denied the most serious allegations.

evidence_headlines.title: 특검, '매관매직' 김건희에 징역 7년 6개월 구형‥"대통령 영향력 거래 수단으로 이용"

topics.summary: Yoon Suk-yeol was sentenced to life imprisonment for insurrection in February 2026, and received 7 years on an arrest-obstruction appeal in late April; a further insurrection-related sentencing is scheduled for May 28. The PPP continues to distance itself from Yoon but faces internal pressure from pro-Yoon factions that received by-election nominations.

evidence_headlines.title: Yoon Suk Yeol: Former South Korean President handed life sentence for leading insurrection

topics.summary: The Democratic Party introduced legislation to appoint a special counsel to investigate alleged fabricated prosecutions by the Yoon-era justice apparatus, including cases against President Lee Jae-myung; the bill controversially grants the special counsel power to withdraw indictments (공소취소권). Legal scholars, the PPP, and some within the DPK itself have raised constitutional objections, and the controversy contributed to a 3-point dip in Lee's approval rating.

evidence_headlines.title: 민주당 "조작기소 바로잡을 특검 필요" vs 국민의힘 "셀프 면죄부"
조작기소 특검 논란 확산…법조계·학계 "거꾸로 특검" "헌정 질서 훼손"
與, '공소취소권 부여' 조작기소 특검법 발의…野 "셀프면죄"(종합)

topics.topic_en: PPP Internal Divisions Ahead of Elections
topics.topic_ko: 국민의힘 내분 및 친윤 공천 논란
topics.summary: PPP leader Jang Dong-hyuk faces resignation calls from within the party as the 'jeol-yun' (severing ties with Yoon) declaration collides with by-election nominations awarded to pro-Yoon figures. Former PPP leader Han Dong-hoon is running in a Busan by-election in a three-way statistical tie, with some pro-Han lawmakers openly defying party leadership.

Seoul served as the venue for pre-summit talks between US Treasury Secretary Bessent and China's Vice Premier He Lifeng on May 13, ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing (May 14-15). President Lee Jae-myung met both delegations, proposed a Korea-US currency swap, and framed Korea as a constructive middle-ground diplomatic actor.

evidence_headlines.title: 미중 무역협상 무대 된 韓…李대통령, 美엔 공급망·中엔 실질협력 강조
미중 회담 서울서 전초전‥이 대통령 잇단 접견

topics.topic_en: Lee Jae-myung Approval Slips to 61 Percent
topics.summary: The latest Gallup Korea poll (May 12-14) shows President Lee Jae-myung's approval at 61%, down 3 points from two weeks prior, with analysts attributing the slip to public ambivalence over the fabricated-prosecution special counsel bill; the DPK trails at 45% party support versus 23% for the PPP, which has risen for four consecutive weeks.

evidence_headlines.title: 이 대통령 지지율 61%...TK 10%p 하락, 조작기소 특검 탓?

topics.topic_en: Constitutional Amendment Referendum Blocked by PPP
topics.summary: A multi-party constitutional amendment bill—featuring stronger martial-law restrictions and enshrining the May 18 Gwangju democracy movement—failed to pass because the PPP boycotted the National Assembly vote needed to schedule a simultaneous referendum on June 3; surveys showed 59% of the public supported the concurrent vote, and the DPK is using the blockade as an election-season narrative against the PPP.

evidence_headlines.title: 제9회 전국동시지방선거 — 개헌 국민투표 동시실시 무산

method_notes: Captured via Anthropic API (Sonnet 4.6) with web_search for live Korean political coverage. Topic labels (topic_en) are stable handles for cross-snapshot rank comparison.
